response:
Deploying a deep learning model on AWS requires careful planning and execution. * * *  
Model training is conducted on-premises or in the cloud, with data preprocessing and model tuning performed before deployment. *  
Data security is paramount, with AWS offering encryption and access controls to protect sensitive information. * * *  
Model deployment involves packaging the model and deploying it on AWS services like SageMaker or EC2. *  
Overfitting can occur if the model is too complex or the training data is insufficient, requiring regularization techniques. *  
AWS provides monitoring tools to track performance and detect anomalies, ensuring the model remains effective. * * *  
Security measures include IAM roles and encrypted storage, while overfitting is mitigated through techniques like dropout and early stopping. *  
AWS supports version control and rollback mechanisms, ensuring model integrity and compliance. *
